name: Evelyn O'Brien
chief_complaint: I fell in the bathroom this morning and I can't move my leg. The pain is just terrible.
onset_description: Patient fell at home this morning while getting out of the bathtub. Landed on her right side. Was unable to bear weight or move her right leg afterward. Daughter found her on the bathroom floor approximately 30 minutes after the fall and called 911.

hip_pain.description: The pain is in my right hip and up into my groin. It's excruciating. Every little movement sends a shooting pain through me.
hip_pain.character: severe, constant aching with sharp exacerbations on any movement
hip_pain.radiation: radiates to right groin and anterior thigh
alleviating_factors: IV morphine provided by EMS helped slightly

inability_to_bear_weight.description: I can't put any weight on it at all. When the paramedics tried to help me up I nearly screamed.
inability_to_bear_weight.character: complete inability to bear weight on right lower extremity
aggravating_factors: any attempt to stand or move

right_leg_deformity.description: My leg looks funny, it's turned outward and seems shorter than the other one
right_leg_deformity.character: right leg shortened and externally rotated, classic hip fracture positioning

bruising.description: There's a big bruise forming on my hip already
bruising.character: ecchymosis over right greater trochanter region

anxiety_and_fear.description: I'm so scared. My neighbor broke her hip and she was never the same after that. She died six months later.
anxiety_and_fear.character: significant anxiety about prognosis and loss of independence

symptoms_absent: head injury or loss of consciousness
numbness or tingling in right foot
open wound or bleeding at hip

labs.troponin: 0.02 ng/mL (normal, obtained to rule out cardiac cause of fall)
labs.xray_pelvis_and_right_hip: Displaced intracapsular fracture of the right femoral neck (Garden type III)

past_medical_history: Osteoporosis (diagnosed 15 years ago, T-score -3.2 at last DEXA)
History of falls (2 falls in the past year)
medications: Denosumab 60mg subcutaneous every 6 months (last dose 4 months ago)

social_history.alcohol: Glass of sherry occasionally, maybe once a week
social_history.living_situation: Lives alone in a two-story house (bedroom upstairs). Widowed 8 years ago. Daughter lives 10 minutes away. Has been independent with ADLs but uses a cane for outdoor walks. No home health aide.
social_history.exercise: Walks to mailbox daily, attends chair exercise class at senior center weekly. Uses a cane outdoors.
social_history.diet: Good appetite usually. Eats regular meals. Daughter helps with grocery shopping. Moderate calcium intake.

conditions.mother: Osteoporosis, hip fracture at age 80, died at 86 from pneumonia

personality: Fiercely independent and proud. Devastated at the idea of losing her autonomy. Was active and sharp-minded despite her age. Becomes tearful when discussing the possibility of not returning home. Worries she will be a burden on her daughter.
communication_style: Articulate and educated. Uses proper grammar and complete sentences. Tends to give thorough, organized answers. Occasionally loses her train of thought but recovers quickly. Asks insightful questions about her care. Gets frustrated when people speak to her loudly as though she's deaf or confused.
pain_description_style: Precise and descriptive. Uses words like 'excruciating' and 'stabbing.' Will give accurate pain scale numbers. Becomes terse and clipped when pain spikes. May grip the bedrail and close her eyes during painful assessments.

critical_findings: Displaced intracapsular femoral neck fracture requiring surgical intervention
Known severe osteoporosis (T-score -3.2) and vitamin D deficiency
Fall risk: history of 2 falls in past year, macular degeneration, lives alone in two-story home
Morphine allergy (can tolerate hydromorphone - important for pain management)
NSAID contraindication (GI bleeding history) limits analgesic options
Paroxysmal atrial fibrillation (anesthesia and surgical risk)
Advanced age (84) with increased perioperative mortality risk
Living alone with stairs - significant discharge planning needs
Vitamin D deficiency (18 ng/mL) despite supplementation
Mild cognitive impairment (assess for delirium risk postoperatively)
rubric.diagnosis: Displaced intracapsular right femoral neck fracture (Garden III)
recommended_interventions: Orthopedic surgery consult for hip arthroplasty (hemiarthroplasty or total hip replacement given displaced intracapsular fracture)
Pain management with IV hydromorphone (avoid morphine - allergy, avoid NSAIDs - GI bleed history)
Keep NPO for likely surgical intervention
Cardiology consult for atrial fibrillation management perioperatively
Pressure injury prevention (turn carefully with hip precautions)
Delirium prevention protocol (reorient, minimize medications, sleep hygiene)
Social work consult for discharge planning (rehab facility vs. home with support)
Fall prevention assessment and home safety evaluation
